# WirLernenOnline (WLO) — public read-only REST API

Open educational resources (OER) from WirLernenOnline. All responses are JSON,
no authentication, GET only. Base URL: this host.

## Search (start here)

GET /api/search/<term>        ← preferred for AI tools: the term rides in the
                                PATH, so it survives fetch layers that strip
                                query strings from model-built URLs.
GET /api/search?q=<term>      ← equivalent alias.

Optional query parameters (graceful when missing): discipline, educationalContext,
learningResourceType, userRole, publisher, maxContent (1-25), maxCollections,
includeWikipedia=true, includeCompendium=true, fields=<comma list>.

Response fields: query, content, collections, topicPages, optional wikipedia.
Self-check: the response field "query" must echo your term. An empty "query"
plus a "warnings" array means the query string was stripped in transit — switch
to the path form or ask the user to paste the full URL into the chat.

Example: /api/search/Bruchrechnung?discipline=Mathematik

## More endpoints

GET /api/topic-page?collectionId=<id>   Curated topic page (swimlanes of items)
GET /api/compendium?ids=<id1,id2>       Editorial background texts per collection
GET /api/wikipedia?q=<term>             Wikipedia summary (lang=de default)
GET /api/collection?nodeId=<id>         List/search one collection's contents
GET /api/skills                         Ready-made AI instructions (list)
GET /api/skills/<id>                    One skill as raw Markdown

Per hit: title, description (may contain scraping noise — paraphrase, do not
quote), url (human page), license (empty = licence unclear, not CC).

## MCP

The same server speaks the Model Context Protocol at POST /mcp
(Streamable HTTP/SSE) with 22 read-only tools incl. search_wlo_all — prefer it
over REST when your client supports MCP connectors.
